tweets.text:
Software horror: litellm PyPI supply chain attack. 

Simple `pip install litellm` was enough to exfiltrate SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure creds, Kubernetes configs, git credentials, env vars (all your API keys), shell history, crypto wallets, SSL private keys, CI/CD secrets, database passwords.

LiteLLM itself has 97 million downloads per month which is already terrible, but much worse, the contagion spreads to any project that depends on litellm. For example, if you did `pip install dspy` (which depended on litellm>=1.64.0), you'd also be pwnd. Same for any other large project that depended on litellm.

Afaict the poisoned version was up for only less than ~1 hour. The attack had a bug which led to its discovery - Callum McMahon was using an MCP plugin inside Cursor that pulled in litellm as a transitive dependency. When litellm 1.82.8 installed, their machine ran out of RAM and crashed. So if the attacker didn't vibe code this attack it could have been undetected for many days or weeks.

Supply chain attacks like this are basically the scariest thing imaginable in modern software. Every time you install any depedency you could be pulling in a poisoned package anywhere deep inside its entire depedency tree. This is especially risky with large projects that might have lots and lots of dependencies. The credentials that do get stolen in each attack can then be used to take over more accounts and compromise more packages.

Classical software engineering would have you believe that dependencies are good (we're building pyramids from bricks), but imo this has to be re-evaluated, and it's why I've been so growingly averse to them, preferring to use LLMs to "yoink" functionality when it's simple enough and possible.
tweets.summary_zh:
最近有个软件安全事件，叫做 litellm 的供应链攻击。简单来说，只要运行一个命令，就能盗取你的很多信息，比如 SSH 密钥、云服务的账户密码和数据库密码等等。

这个 litellm 每个月有 9700 万次下载，意义重大。如果你安装了依赖于 litellm 的其他软件，也可能被波及。虽然这个被攻击的版本只存在了一小会儿就被发现了，但这种供应链攻击在现代软件中非常可怕，因为你每次安装软件都有可能拉进恶意的包。

传统软件开发认为依赖是有益的，但我觉得现在得重新考虑这个问题，所以我更倾向于用大型语言模型（LLM）来提取功能，而不是依赖太多其他的库。

tweets.text:
Vercel can now intelligently pick the right hardware for your build.

With new Rust-based compilers like Turbopack & Rolldown, build performance now scales with 𝒪(cpus).

But too many CPUs and you waste money. Too few and agents waste time. Elastic build machines fixes this 😌 https://t.co/W5oQ906TxX
tweets.summary_zh: Vercel 现在可以智能地选择适合你项目的硬件，提升构建（build）效率。新推出的基于 Rust 的工具让性能更好，但要找到合适的 CPU 数量很重要，太多会浪费钱，太少又会拖慢进度。弹性构建机器解决了这个问题，让一切变得更轻松。

tweets.text:
Almost every SaaS app inside Vercel has now been replaced with a generated app or agent interface, deployed on Vercel.

Support, sales, marketing, PM, HR, dataviz, even design and video workflows. It’s shocking.

The SaaSpocalypse is both understated and overstated. Over because the key systems of record and storage are still there (Salesforce, Snowflake, etc.)

Understated because the software we are generating is more beautiful, personalized, and crucially, fits our business problems better. 

We struggled for years to represent the health of a Vercel customer properly inside Salesforce. Too much data (trillions of consumption data points), the ontology of Vercel was a mismatch to the built-in assumptions, and the resulting UI was bizarre. We generated what we needed instead. When you don’t need a UI, you just ask an agent with natural language.

We’ve also been moving off legacy systems with poor, slow, outdated, and inconsistent APIs, as well as just dropping abstraction down to more traditional databases. UI is a function 𝑓 of data (always has been), and that 𝑓 is increasingly becoming the LLM.

“软件大变革”这个说法有点夸大也有点保守。夸大是因为关键的记录和存储系统（比如 Salesforce 和 Snowflake）还是存在的；保守则是因为我们生成的软件更加美观、个性化，更重要的是，它更好地解决了我们的业务问题。

tweets.text:
“We’ve also been moving off legacy systems with poor, slow, outdated, and inconsistent APIs.”

If you’re building software that can’t work fully headlessly in a way that agents want to use, you’re not prepared for what the future of software is going to look like. 

Agents will use software 100X more than people, and people will more and more interact with their data and workflows via agents across many different platforms.

This is the real risk but also opportunity for platforms right now. Software doesn’t go away, but it becomes the guardrails and business logic for what agents are able to operate on. But if you can’t connect to wherever the agents want to do that work, you’re DOA.
tweets.summary_zh: 我们正在放弃那些慢、过时、以及不一致的老系统，因为未来的软件必须能让智能代理（像自动化程序）全方位使用。如果你的软件不能适应这种趋势，那你就落后了。智能代理将比人类使用软件的频率高出100倍，未来人们会通过这些代理在多个平台上处理数据和工作。这个变化带来了风险，也意味着新的机会。如果你的软件不能很好地与这些智能代理连接，那就太危险了。

tweets.text:
Software horror: litellm PyPI supply chain attack. 

Simple `pip install litellm` was enough to exfiltrate SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure creds, Kubernetes configs, git credentials, env vars (all your API keys), shell history, crypto wallets, SSL private keys, CI/CD secrets, database passwords.

LiteLLM itself has 97 million downloads per month which is already terrible, but much worse, the contagion spreads to any project that depends on litellm. For example, if you did `pip install dspy` (which depended on litellm>=1.64.0), you'd also be pwnd. Same for any other large project that depended on litellm.

Afaict the poisoned version was up for only less than ~1 hour. The attack had a bug which led to its discovery - Callum McMahon was using an MCP plugin inside Cursor that pulled in litellm as a transitive dependency. When litellm 1.82.8 installed, their machine ran out of RAM and crashed. So if the attacker didn't vibe code this attack it could have been undetected for many days or weeks.

Supply chain attacks like this are basically the scariest thing imaginable in modern software. Every time you install any depedency you could be pulling in a poisoned package anywhere deep inside its entire depedency tree. This is especially risky with large projects that might have lots and lots of dependencies. The credentials that do get stolen in each attack can then be used to take over more accounts and compromise more packages.

Classical software engineering would have you believe that dependencies are good (we're building pyramids from bricks), but imo this has to be re-evaluated, and it's why I've been so growingly averse to them, preferring to use LLMs to "yoink" functionality when it's simple enough and possible.
tweets.summary_zh:
最近有个软件安全事件，叫做 litellm 的供应链攻击。简单来说，只要运行一个命令，就能盗取你的很多信息，比如 SSH 密钥、云服务的账户密码和数据库密码等等。

这个 litellm 每个月有 9700 万次下载，意义重大。如果你安装了依赖于 litellm 的其他软件，也可能被波及。虽然这个被攻击的版本只存在了一小会儿就被发现了，但这种供应链攻击在现代软件中非常可怕，因为你每次安装软件都有可能拉进恶意的包。

传统软件开发认为依赖是有益的，但我觉得现在得重新考虑这个问题，所以我更倾向于用大型语言模型（LLM）来提取功能，而不是依赖太多其他的库。

tweets.text:
Vercel can now intelligently pick the right hardware for your build.

With new Rust-based compilers like Turbopack & Rolldown, build performance now scales with 𝒪(cpus).

But too many CPUs and you waste money. Too few and agents waste time. Elastic build machines fixes this 😌 https://t.co/W5oQ906TxX
tweets.summary_zh: Vercel 现在可以智能地选择适合你项目的硬件，提升构建（build）效率。新推出的基于 Rust 的工具让性能更好，但要找到合适的 CPU 数量很重要，太多会浪费钱，太少又会拖慢进度。弹性构建机器解决了这个问题，让一切变得更轻松。

tweets.text:
Almost every SaaS app inside Vercel has now been replaced with a generated app or agent interface, deployed on Vercel.

Support, sales, marketing, PM, HR, dataviz, even design and video workflows. It’s shocking.

The SaaSpocalypse is both understated and overstated. Over because the key systems of record and storage are still there (Salesforce, Snowflake, etc.)

Understated because the software we are generating is more beautiful, personalized, and crucially, fits our business problems better. 

We struggled for years to represent the health of a Vercel customer properly inside Salesforce. Too much data (trillions of consumption data points), the ontology of Vercel was a mismatch to the built-in assumptions, and the resulting UI was bizarre. We generated what we needed instead. When you don’t need a UI, you just ask an agent with natural language.

We’ve also been moving off legacy systems with poor, slow, outdated, and inconsistent APIs, as well as just dropping abstraction down to more traditional databases. UI is a function 𝑓 of data (always has been), and that 𝑓 is increasingly becoming the LLM.
tweets.summary_zh:
几乎所有在 Vercel 的 SaaS（软件即服务）应用现在都被生成的应用或代理界面替代了，这些都是部署在 Vercel 上的。支持、销售、市场、项目管理、人力资源、数据可视化，甚至设计和视频制作工作流程都受到了影响，真是让人震惊。

“软件大变革”这个说法有点夸大也有点保守。夸大是因为关键的记录和存储系统（比如 Salesforce 和 Snowflake）还是存在的；保守则是因为我们生成的软件更加美观、个性化，更重要的是，它更好地解决了我们的业务问题。

tweets.text:
“We’ve also been moving off legacy systems with poor, slow, outdated, and inconsistent APIs.”

If you’re building software that can’t work fully headlessly in a way that agents want to use, you’re not prepared for what the future of software is going to look like. 

Agents will use software 100X more than people, and people will more and more interact with their data and workflows via agents across many different platforms.

This is the real risk but also opportunity for platforms right now. Software doesn’t go away, but it becomes the guardrails and business logic for what agents are able to operate on. But if you can’t connect to wherever the agents want to do that work, you’re DOA.
tweets.summary_zh: 我们正在放弃那些慢、过时、以及不一致的老系统，因为未来的软件必须能让智能代理（像自动化程序）全方位使用。如果你的软件不能适应这种趋势，那你就落后了。智能代理将比人类使用软件的频率高出100倍，未来人们会通过这些代理在多个平台上处理数据和工作。这个变化带来了风险，也意味着新的机会。如果你的软件不能很好地与这些智能代理连接，那就太危险了。

tweets.text:
Computer use and the ability to write and run code on the fly are the ultimate primitives for agents to be able to take on more and more tasks in knowledge work. 

Most work requires hopping between multiple applications, and working with broad sets of data, in a workflow, and agents will need to be able to traverse these systems to be able to effectively automate any real work in the enterprise. 

Now we will have agents that are the equivalent of having an expert programmer (or any number of them) that can write code or use any API to automate whatever work you’re doing. Agents will have access to either a user’s computer and resources, or their own sandbox to operate in, and be able to pull together the tools necessary to perform the task at hand. This opens up the broadest set of agentic use-cases.

To be sure, there are going to be various hurdles around security, permissions and access controls, identity challenges, and more. 

For instance, should the agent always act on behalf of the user, or should they have their own identity and limited set of access rights? How do you triage security events when historically volume of activity on a system is no longer a reliable signal of a security issue? How do you ensure the agent isn’t going rogue or getting prompt injected to do something risky? All problems that need to get figured out.

Then, there’s also lots of work needed to ensure software is setup to enable to agents to operate with their tools in a headless fashion. This will be an uncomfortable reality for some incumbents, and equally a welcome one for tools that historically have operated seamlessly via APIs, and have business models to support this.

Lots of change coming in the world of work agents, and it’s going to get pretty wild.
tweets.summary_zh: Computer use（让 AI 操作电脑）和实时写代码/调 API 是 AI Agent 最关键的两个能力。大部分工作都需要在多个应用之间切换，Agent 必须能跨系统操作才能真正自动化企业工作流。不过安全、权限管理、身份验证这些问题还需要解决。这会颠覆很多现有软件格局。

tweets.text:
Pretty much every PR I review:

0) review <URL> [codex does it's thing and finds issues]
1) is the issue clear? [if not, trash PR]
2) is this the best possible fix? [95% of the time no]
3) continue discussion, consider tradeoffs, usually rewrite PR

Most folks send too localized, small fixes that would end up making the project unmaintainable.

producthunt.name: InsForge
producthunt.tagline: Give agents everything they need to ship fullstack apps
producthunt.summary_zh: InsForge 是一个全面的软件开发平台，专为开发人员提供工具和资源，以便他们能够快速构建和部署完整的全栈应用。这个产品集成了代码编辑、数据库管理和云服务，简化了开发流程，让用户能更高效地实现项目。

producthunt.name: Tobira.ai
producthunt.tagline: A network where AI agents find deals for their humans
producthunt.summary_zh: Tobira.ai 是一个智能网络，利用人工智能代理为用户寻找最优交易。用户通过平台可以轻松获取各种商品和服务的优惠信息，从而节省时间和金钱。这个产品旨在简化购物体验，让用户更快速地发现值得购买的优惠。

producthunt.name: Claude Marketplace
producthunt.tagline: Helping companies easily get the AI tools they need
producthunt.summary_zh: Claude Marketplace是一个为企业提供人工智能工具的平台。用户可以在这里轻松找到并获取所需的AI解决方案，以提升工作效率和推动业务发展。该平台汇集了多种AI产品，方便用户比较和选择。

producthunt.name: Lightfield
producthunt.tagline: AI-native CRM that builds itself and does work for you
producthunt.summary_zh: Lightfield是一款基于人工智能的客户关系管理系统（CRM），它能够自动构建和优化自身。用户可以通过它轻松管理客户信息和互动，系统会主动为用户完成一些繁琐的任务，提高工作效率。

producthunt.name: Needle 2.0
producthunt.tagline: Vibe-automate workflows and earn passive income
producthunt.summary_zh: Needle 2.0 是一款自动化工作流程管理工具。通过优化和简化工作流程，用户可以在完成任务的同时获得被动收入。它帮助用户更高效地管理时间和资源。

producthunt.name: Claude Computer Use
producthunt.tagline: Enable Claude to use your computer to complete tasks
producthunt.summary_zh: Claude Computer Use 是一款软件，能够让 Claude 这位智能助手通过你的电脑来完成各种任务。用户只需简单设置，Claude 就能处理文档、搜索信息和执行其他操作，提高工作效率。

producthunt.name: ProductBridge
producthunt.tagline: Agent that collects feedback across multiple platforms
producthunt.summary_zh: ProductBridge 是一款能够在多个平台上收集用户反馈的工具。它帮助企业整合来自不同渠道的意见和建议，以便更好地了解客户需求和提升产品质量。通过简单的设置，用户可以轻松获取和分析反馈信息。

github_trending.description: AI agent skill that researches any topic across Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web - then synthesizes a grounded summary
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个AI工具，可以自动研究Reddit、X、YouTube等网站上的主题，并生成一个综合的总结，帮助用户快速获取信息。对Yves来说，这个工具可以在Logic Labs（AI公司）中用于信息收集与市场调研，提高创意设计时的决策效率，同时也可以帮助TexTale了解市场趋势与消费者反馈，为品牌营销提供数据支持。

github_trending.description: An open-source SuperAgent harness that researches, codes, and creates. With the help of sandboxes, memories, tools, skill, subagents and message gateway, it handles different levels of tasks that could take minutes to hours.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个开源工具，能够利用AI帮助用户执行复杂的任务，比如编程、创作等。对于Yves的Logic Labs（AI公司），这个项目可以帮助他们更高效地开发AI应用和解决方案，而对于TexTale（科技服装电商），它也可以在产品设计和市场营销中提供创意支持。作为创意设计从业者，Yves可以借助这个工具激发灵感，提升工作效率。

github_trending.description: Python SDK, Proxy Server (AI Gateway) to call 100+ LLM APIs in OpenAI (or native) format, with cost tracking, guardrails, loadbalancing and logging. [Bedrock, Azure, OpenAI, VertexAI, Cohere, Anthropic, Sagemaker, HuggingFace, VLLM, NVIDIA NIM]
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个Python软件开发工具包，帮助用户通过一个统一的接口调用多个大型语言模型（LLM）API，同时提供费用追踪、流量管理和日志记录等功能。对Yves来说，这个项目可以帮助Logic Labs更高效地整合和管理不同的AI工具，提升AI应用的开发速度和成本控制能力，同时也能为TexTale在品牌营销和客户互动上提供先进的智能化服务。
这个项目是一个开源的在线编辑器，专注于提供用户友好的界面和功能，帮助人们更方便地创建和编辑内容。对于Yves来说，这个工具可以帮助TexTale在电商平台上快速生成高质量的产品描述和营销材料，同时也能够为Logic Labs的AI产品提供更直观的用户交互界面，提升创意设计的效率。

github_trending.description: 🌊 The leading agent orchestration platform for Claude. Deploy intelligent multi-agent swarms, coordinate autonomous workflows, and build conversational AI systems. Features enterprise-grade architecture, distributed swarm intelligence, RAG integration, and native Claude Code / Codex Integration
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个智能代理协作平台，旨在帮助开发者更高效地部署和管理多个智能代理，以实现自动化工作流程和对话式人工智能系统。对Yves的Logic Labs（AI公司）特别有帮助，因为这个平台能够提升他在AI项目中的工作效率，同时增强创意设计的实现能力。对于TexTale（科技服装电商），虽然直接关联不大，但也可以潜在地优化客户服务和营销自动化。

github_trending.description: Project N.O.M.A.D, is a self-contained, offline survival computer packed with critical tools, knowledge, and AI to keep you informed and empowered—anytime, anywhere.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个便携的离线生存电脑，里面包含了重要的工具和知识，利用人工智能在任何地方都能帮助用户获取信息和提升能力。对Yves的Logic Labs（AI公司）可能更有帮助，因为这个项目展示了如何将AI应用于实际问题，激发他在创意设计中的新思路，同时对于TexTale则可以启发他在科技服装中的创新应用。

github_trending.description: π RuView: WiFi DensePose turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video.
github_trending.summary_zh:
这个项目利用普通的WiFi信号来实时估计人的姿势、监测生命体征和检测是否有人存在，而无需使用任何视频拍摄。这项技术可以在不侵犯隐私的情况下获取用户的行为和健康数据。

对Yves来说，这项技术可以为Logic Labs的AI研究提供前沿的应用案例，并为TexTale的电商平台提供创新的用户体验，帮助优化客户互动与个性化服务，同时提升品牌形象。作为创意设计从业者，RuView的应用可以激发他在产品设计和功能构思上的创新灵感。

github_trending.description: Memory engine and app that is extremely fast, scalable. The Memory API for the AI era.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个内存引擎和应用程序，能够快速且高效地存储和管理信息，特别适合人工智能时代的需求。对于Yves而言，Logic Labs（AI 公司）可以利用这个内存解决方案提高AI模型的处理能力和效率，同时TexTale（科技服装电商）也可以在数据处理和用户体验上受益，帮助他更好地进行品牌营销和创意设计。

github_trending.description: Automate the process of making money online.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目旨在自动化在线赚钱的过程，帮助用户找到并实现各种获取收入的方法。对Yves来说，这个项目可能对TexTale（科技服装电商）更有帮助，因为能够利用其中的自动化工具来优化电商运营，提升收入效率。同时，这也为他作为创意设计从业者提供了灵感，探索如何将智能化手段应用于品牌营销。

github_trending.description: Open-source AI hackers to find and fix your app’s vulnerabilities.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个开源工具，旨在帮助开发者发现和修复应用程序中的安全漏洞。对Yves来说，Logic Labs（AI公司）可以利用这个工具提高AI应用的安全性，而TexTale（科技服装电商）则能确保其电商平台在保护用户数据和交易安全方面更加可靠，同时也能提升品牌形象。作为创意设计从业者，了解安全工具的应用可以让Yves在设计阶段考虑更多实际需求，增强产品的安全感和信任度。
